_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Inclusion involves including everyone to incorporate disparate views and requirements, whether developing products and services or internal policies.
reference_document_element_description: Design and execute research projects that advance organizational AI capability, including hypothesis formulation, experimental design, execution, and findings documentation.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-T-018 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-T-004 (LS, cosine: 0.391), AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.371).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 1.9/10 (raw 0.192). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DDaT-only terms [developing, disparate, everyone, inclusion, incorporate, internal] vs WIDAI-only terms [advance, ai, capability, design, documentation, execute] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.849 (P=0.852, R=0.845). Balanced P/R — the 18-word DDaT element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (98.2%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Inclusion involves including everyone to incorporate disparate views and require" and "Design and execute research projects that advance organizational AI capability,". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.341 > STS 0.192 (gap +0.149). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.2%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DDaT terms: [developing, disparate, everyone, inclusion, incorporate, internal]. WIDAI terms: [advance, ai, capability, design, documentation, execute].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 98.2% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.149 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
